heading: Supported Conversions
body:
Atlas Data Federation converts the partition attributes to [BSON types](http://bsonspec.org/spec.html) when parsing the
[path](datalakeconf-aws.databases.\[n].collections.\[n].dataSources.\[n].path)
to the filename. Later writes of data to cloud storage must use the BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) types
after converting them to string. The following table shows:

- The partition attribute types and the BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) types to which Data Federation converts
  them.

- The BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) data type to convert to a string for later writes to cloud storage.

| Partition Attribute Type | Parsed BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) Type                      | Source BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) Type                      |
| ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| string                   | UTF-8 stringnull\*                                                        | UTF-8 stringnull                                                          |
| int                      | 64-bit integernull                                                        | 32-bit integer64-bit integernull (as strings with no padding)             |
| isodate                  | UTC datetimenull                                                          | UTC datetime (as an ISO-8601 format string)null                           |
| objectid                 | ObjectIdnull                                                              | ObjectId (as a string with hex encoding)null                              |
| uuid                     | BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) Binary data subtype 4 (UUID)null | BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) Binary data subtype 4 (UUID)null |
